sources.sourceTitle: Introduction to Algorithms (Cormen, Leiserson, Rivest, Stein), Ch. 2
Open Data Structures (Pat Morin), Ch. 1 — Array-Based Sequences

facts.statement: Binary search requires the input array to be sorted; it does not work correctly on unsorted data.
Binary search runs in O(log n) time, compared to O(n) for linear search, because each comparison eliminates half of the remaining search space.

At each step, binary search compares the target value to the middle element of the current range: if equal, the search ends; if the target is smaller, the search continues on the left half; if larger, it continues on the right half.
Binary search terminates either by finding the target or by the search range becoming empty (low pointer exceeds high pointer), which means the target is not present.
